title: Intense Spicy Vietnamese Pork Pho
description: Rich, cheese-pull, hangover-cure Vietnamese dish featuring cayenne pepper, stone-bowl-dolsot, whole30-friendly.
instructions: Rinse the pork neck bones and pat them dry with paper towels.
Season the pork neck bones with cayenne pepper and place them on a baking sheet.
Bake the pork neck bones in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
While the pork neck bones are baking, heat the fatty beef broth in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, ginger, star anise, and onion to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the broth simmer for 10 minutes.
While the broth is simmering, cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Once the noodles are cooked, drain them and set them aside.
After 10 minutes, add the cooked rice noodles, bean sprouts, and cilantro to the pot.
Simmer the broth for an additional 2 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
To serve, place a portion of the noodles into a stone bowl and ladle the hot broth over the top.
Place a slice of sirloin steak into the bowl and garnish with cilantro and lime wedges.
